Back: The first step in the Conception phase where a project is initially considered.
The stage in the Conception phase used to assess if the project is viable.
Creating the initial proposal document to secure project approval.
Defining the overall execution plan for the project.
Defining the detailed technical requirements for what the end-item must do.
Translating what the user wants into specific technical targets for the system.
The stage where detailed functional and physical designs are created based on system requirements.
The stage where the system is physically fabricated, constructed, or assembled.
The stage focused on user acceptance, training, and installing the final system.
Performing standard, periodic repairs and preventative maintenance on the operational system.
Reviewing the operational system to check if it is delivering the expected benefits to the user.
Enhancing the system or implementing project extensions based on operational feedback.
The final step in the life cycle when the operational system is retired.
A project manager responsibility during the execution phase to bring more staff on board.
The task of delegating specific duties to team members during project execution.
Managing modifications to the project and ensuring the final product meets required standards.
Coordinating different project tasks to ensure they work together smoothly.
A series of checks to ensure designs and manufactured items meet the functional and basic requirements.
A design methodology that distinguishes what a system does (functional) from its physical hardware or software form.
Review checkpoints to verify if a concept fits basic requirements and if the detailed design reflects the functional design.
The actual physical construction, assembly, and manufacturing of the system.
Conducting destructive and non-destructive tests to check the design against requirements.
Creating a detailed plan for how the final product will be rolled out and installed.
Teaching the end-users how to properly operate and use the new system.
Final tests performed to get the user's approval before full system rollout.

Front: Installation strategies: parallel, pilot, cold turkey
Back: Different methods to roll out a new system, such as running old and new together (parallel), testing a small group (pilot), or an instant cutover (cold turkey).
A close-out activity involving moving project team members to new work or projects.
A final close-out activity to officially finish and sign off on all tasks and subcontracted work.
Monitoring the disposition and handover of surplus materials and special project equipment.
A postmortem evaluation of how well the project was managed and whether it met cost and schedule objectives.
Evaluating the fully operational end-item to ensure it is doing what it was intended to do and fulfilling user needs.
An illegal and unethical practice involving forced labor that can occur in the dark side of projects.
Illegal financial practices and bribery that project managers should attempt to resist.
An unethical situation where personal interests improperly influence professional decisions on a project.
Unethical practices like sexism and making false claims about a project's environmental benefits.
